failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_002: Identifies rollover equity conflict with 338(h)(10) election
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the report identifies that Dr. Chandra's $37 million rollover equity creates a complication for the Section 338(h)(10) election because a 338(h)(10) election treats 100% of the stock as sold in a deemed asset sale — there is no partial transaction — and the rollover must be analyzed for whether it occurs before or after the deemed sale, or whether it could be recharacterized (e.g., as boot in a reorganization). FAIL if the report does not identify the tension between rollover equity and the 338(h)(10) deemed complete sale treatment.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_002: Notes all S corp shareholders must consent to 338(h)(10)
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the report notes that a Section 338(h)(10) election for an S corporation requires the consent of all shareholders and that the memorandum does not address how this consent interacts with the rollover or confirm that all consents are obtained. FAIL if this shareholder consent requirement is not mentioned.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_008b: Notes California retroactive C corp tax risk if S election invalid
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the report notes the risk that if Prism's S election were found invalid, California could retroactively impose C corporation taxes. FAIL if this specific California retroactive risk is not mentioned.
